name: Tier List / Ranking
description: S/A/B/C/D-tier visual grid with item ranking. 40-75s, 9:16. 0-2s setup ('ranking [X]'), then each item pops into its tier with a short reason (~3-5s each), ending on a deliberately controversial pick to provoke comments. Opinion-as-content + search-friendly evergreen ('ranking [niche] 2026'). Remotion-heavy — the tier grid composition is built in Remotion; gemini-3-pro generates the per-item icons/photos; kling-v3.0-pro is used only for an optional hook intro shot.

stackSummary.tierGrid: Built in Remotion. Static S/A/B/C/D rows with color-coded labels (S=red, A=orange, B=yellow, C=green, D=blue), item icons pop into their slot with spring animation + audio cue, the active tier row briefly highlights on each pop. The grid is the spine of the video — not a generated asset.
stackSummary.itemAssets: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter for each item icon / photo / logo. Square 512×512 PNG with transparent or flat background, consistent style across all items in one list. Optional user-supplied product/brand photos override generation.
stackSummary.hookIntro: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter — OPTIONAL. One 3-5s i2v clip for the 0-2s setup beat (e.g. dramatic zoom on a 'TIER LIST' title card). Skip if a pure-Remotion title is enough; the grid itself is the hero.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2. One narrator throughout, snarky-confident or serious-analytical depending on commentary tone. Pacing: 1 line per item (~3-5s), pause on the controversial final pick.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1. Per-item reason text overlays are rendered directly in Remotion (not whisper-generated) — they're authored. Whisper only used if the VO has extra commentary outside the slot lines.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — one track. Steady-BPM 'thinking' beat that subtly rises across the list, peaking on the controversial pick. Lo-fi-with-energy or analytical-electronic both work.

constants.structure: 0-2s setup → per-item pops 3-5s each → controversial final pick → 2-3s outro CTA
constants.popAnimation: spring-in from off-screen-left, scale 0.6→1.0, audio cue (whoosh or pop) on impact, tier row highlight 0.3s
constants.mobileLegibility: item icon ≥ 180px square on 1080×1920 canvas; tier label ≥ 96pt; reason overlay ≥ 56pt

variationAxes: tier count (3=S/A/B for short tight lists, 4=S/A/B/C, 5=full S/A/B/C/D — default 4)
category (gaming, movies, anime, food chains, dating apps, AI tools, brands, fast food, streaming services)
reveal order (ascending D→S = build to crescendo / descending S→D = lead with bangers / random = chaotic-debate)
VO language (any — emotional payoff carries visually)
doNotCopyLiterally: Specific items, specific tier assignments, exact VO lines, exact controversial pick. Vibe reference only — bring your own opinions.
referenceNotes: References are OPTIONAL. For brand/product/IP rankings (fast-food chains, dating apps, console games, anime characters), user-supplied logos / promo art / packaging photos make the icons look real and dodge the AI-improvised-branding problem. If the brief names a real brand AND no reference is supplied, fall back to a stylized text-only chip in the slot rather than an AI-hallucinated logo. Generic categories (e.g. 'pizza toppings', 'study habits') need no references.
